title: The Incident

jokes.text: Something spilled in the bed... there's wet in the bed
jokes.setup: Mysterious wetness in bed, partner confused about what happened
jokes.punchline: Building tension toward the reveal it's not a spill
jokes.explanation: Good setup building mystery and tension, David's panic sells the embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, there's something wet in the bed.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: What? I didn't spill anything.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, it's not just on your side.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Are you suggesting that I—

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not suggesting anything. I'm stating a fact.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, I didn't have an accident.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Then what would you call this?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I don't know. Did you check if the dog got in the bed?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We don't have a dog, Patrick.

jokes.text: Oh my God, David, did you wet the bed?
jokes.setup: All the previous confusion about mysterious wetness
jokes.punchline: Direct revelation of the embarrassing truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the buildup, Patrick's matter-of-fact delivery contrasts with David's mortification

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, I need to tell you something. The wetness... it was me. I was so nervous about meeting your family that I actually... I sweated through my pants.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, no.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Yes. Profusely.

jokes.text: Do we have to file for divorce if we were never married?
jokes.setup: David's extreme embarrassment about bed-wetting
jokes.punchline: Illogical but emotionally true desire to escape the relationship
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures David's dramatic nature and the absurdity of his shame spiral

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I cannot believe you told Patrick about the bed-wetting incident.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, it was one time, and it was years ago.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It doesn't matter. Now he knows I'm fundamentally broken.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You're not broken. Everyone has embarrassing things.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Not like this. I'm leaving you.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: What? Over this?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes. It's the only logical solution to my shame.

jokes.text: We are not getting divorced... Yet. I need to see if I can get the stain out of my sheets first.
jokes.setup: David's dramatic divorce request
jokes.punchline: Patrick's practical priorities and mock consideration
jokes.explanation: Patrick's dry humor and practicality perfectly contrasts David's drama

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, if you're getting divorced, we should probably talk about the store. Like, who gets it? Because I've put a lot of work into that place.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, I haven't even told Patrick yet.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Right, okay. Well, when you do, maybe lead with the store thing. Really softens the blow.

jokes.text: Because I'm about to jump out of it!
jokes.setup: David asks if window opens
jokes.punchline: Dramatic threat to jump rather than face embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Classic David dramatics, though somewhat predictable for his character

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Does this window open?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Because if it does, I'm jumping out of it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not staying in this town for another second. I'd rather die.

jokes.text: I will take a shower, but we must never see each other again.
jokes.setup: Patrick trying to be helpful and practical
jokes.punchline: David's continued dramatic overreaction
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of David's shame, the formality of 'we must never' is funny

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'm just trying to help. Maybe we could look at some more affordable options?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Affordable? Patrick, I don't think you understand. This is a catastrophe of biblical proportions. My entire life as I know it is over.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: It's just a budget adjustment.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Just a budget adjustment? JUST? Do you have any idea what you're asking of me? I might have to shop at a store where they don't know my name. Where they don't greet me by my preferred pronouns. This is social death, Patrick. I'm as good as gone.

jokes.text: I love you. / I'm glad one of us does!
jokes.setup: Patrick's sweet declaration of love during crisis
jokes.punchline: David's self-deprecating response maintaining his shame spiral
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment subverted by David's continued self-loathing, good emotional comedy

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I love you. I love you so much.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, that's... that's very sweet, but I don't think you understand what you're saying. I'm a very difficult person. I'm selfish, I'm vain, I'm judgmental...

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No, you don't. You really don't.

jokes.text: So how was your seventh shower?
jokes.setup: Time has passed, David has been obsessively cleaning
jokes.punchline: Specific number emphasizes David's compulsive behavior
jokes.explanation: Good callback to David's obsessive cleaning, number specificity adds humor

jokes.text: because I peed in it!
jokes.setup: David trying to avoid talking about it, Patrick pushing conversation
jokes.punchline: David's blunt, mortified confession
jokes.explanation: Direct confession maintains the cringe factor, David's shame is palpable

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, just talk to me. What's going on?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't want to discuss this.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: We need to talk about it.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: There is nothing to talk about.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David...

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Fine. I cried during the opening credits of a baking show. Are you happy?

jokes.text: I found this blanket that I thought I had lost in the laundry room, so you've actually done me a favour.
jokes.setup: David expecting judgment or awkwardness
jokes.punchline: Patrick finding a silver lining in the bed-wetting incident
jokes.explanation: Patrick's relentless positivity in face of embarrassment is very funny

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: This is so embarrassing. You're going to think I'm disgusting.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, I don't think that at all. Look, everyone has accidents. At least now we know your body is still capable of surprises.

jokes.text: Maybe, but if the tables were turned, I can't say I'd be as generous.
jokes.setup: Patrick's generosity about the bed-wetting
jokes.punchline: David's self-aware admission of his own pettiness
jokes.explanation: David's honesty about his own character flaws is endearing and funny

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I appreciate Patrick's generosity, but let's be honest, I would never let that go. I would bring it up constantly. 'Oh, you want to go out tonight? Remember when you wet the bed?'

jokes.text: You put down a plastic sheet?
jokes.setup: Patrick's weak denials about the crinkling sound
jokes.punchline: David realizing Patrick prepared for this possibility
jokes.explanation: Great reveal that adds new layer to David's mortification

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Wait, is that... did you prepare for this?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I don't know what you're talking about.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You bought crinkly pants. On purpose. You planned ahead for a moment where you might need to deny something loudly.

jokes.text: Purely coincidental.
jokes.setup: Clear evidence Patrick prepared for bed-wetting
jokes.punchline: Patrick's obvious lie delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of obviously untrue statement

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I just like to sleep on a towel. It's very absorbent. Great for moisture.

jokes.text: Come on, David, it's an expensive mattress!
jokes.setup: David horrified by the preparation
jokes.punchline: Patrick's practical justification
jokes.explanation: Patrick's practicality vs David's emotion continues to be funny dynamic

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, what are you doing? You're preparing the salmon like you're performing surgery.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, if we're going to serve it, we should do it properly. Besides, you never know when a fish bone could become a liability.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It's dinner, not a courtroom.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Exactly. Which is why we prepare it correctly.

jokes.text: More expensive than my dignity? / I mean, comparable, at least.
jokes.setup: David's dramatic question about dignity vs mattress cost
jokes.punchline: Patrick's mock consideration of the comparison
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with perfect character dynamics, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: So you're telling me that my dignity is worth less than a mattress?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, I mean, a good mattress can last you ten years...

jokes.text: Takeover? That sounds hostile.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining social media takeover
jokes.punchline: Moira misinterpreting the corporate term
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Moira's interpretation of modern terms

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So basically, a corporation is taking over my social media accounts for the day to show their followers what I do.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, how wonderful! A hostile takeover of your digital presence. I had one of those once. Very traumatic. They took my wig collection and my entire skincare line.

jokes.text: I've dated enough mid-level latte art influencers to know what the people want.
jokes.setup: Alexis claiming social media expertise
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and believable dating history for Alexis
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd and specific to Alexis's character, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, I basically invented Instagram. Well, not invented, but I was definitely an early adopter. I had like 50,000 followers before it was even a thing.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That doesn't make sense.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, well I dated a guy who worked at Facebook, so I basically knew Mark Zuckerberg. And then I dated his roommate, who was a photographer, so that's basically Instagram. And then I dated an influencer before influencers were even a thing. So yeah, I invented social media.

jokes.text: I take a slightly more cerebral approach.
jokes.setup: Discussion of different comedic styles
jokes.punchline: Moira's pretentious self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira pretension, though somewhat expected for her character

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I don't do slapstick. I'm more of a physical comedian in the vein of Greta Garbo or a silent film ingénue who communicates through meaningful glances and the strategic placement of her hands.

jokes.text: an amusement park for clinical narcissists
jokes.setup: Moira's previous description of social media
jokes.punchline: Elaborate and judgmental metaphor
jokes.explanation: Excellent Moira vocabulary and social commentary, very quotable

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Social media is just a series of portals for people to project their curated delusions onto an audience of strangers who are equally invested in maintaining their own fictional narratives.

jokes.text: Big news coming soon, caw caw!
jokes.setup: Professional social media post needed
jokes.punchline: Alexis's juvenile approach to crow movie promotion
jokes.explanation: Shows generational/taste gap between Alexis and Moira

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, so I'm thinking for the crow movie, we do a really fun, engaging social media post. Something like... 'CAW CAW! This movie is gonna be LIT! Get ready to have your minds BLOWN by these sexy crows!' With like, a bunch of emojis.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, no.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: What? It's fun! It's relatable! Crows are having a moment!

jokes.text: let's put a beak on that winking cartoon
jokes.setup: Moira trying to improve Alexis's emoji use
jokes.punchline: Moira's misunderstanding of how emojis work
jokes.explanation: Good technology gap humor with Moira trying to customize emojis

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, darling, your emoji usage is positively pedestrian. You simply must elevate your digital communication.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, I think I'm fine with emojis.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Nonsense. Here, I've composed a message using only the most sophisticated emojis. *reads from phone* Eggplant, peach, water droplets, followed by a microphone.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, no!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: What? I'm simply describing last night's ratatouille. It was followed by a rather impressive vocal performance.

jokes.text: Ashley Tisdale and I played suffragettes... 'You Go, Girl.'
jokes.setup: Moira reminiscing about past projects
jokes.punchline: Historically serious subject with vapid title
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between serious historical topic and silly show title

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I once did a historical drama about the French Revolution called 'Let Them Eat Spray Tan.'

jokes.text: I believe we've just gone viral.
jokes.setup: Five likes on social media post
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic overstatement of minor engagement
jokes.explanation: Moira's inflated sense of social media success is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Five likes? FIVE?! Do you understand what this means? I have officially gone viral. I am a social media sensation. *This* is how it starts. Tomorrow, sponsorships. Next week, a Netflix deal. I'm already mentally preparing my acceptance speech for Influencer of the Year.

jokes.text: I think you and I might need to purchase... antibiotics.
jokes.setup: Claiming they've 'gone viral'
jokes.punchline: Moira taking viral literally and medically
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'viral' with perfect Moira medical misunderstanding

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We've gone viral.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mother, that's not what that means.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I don't appreciate your tone, David. I'm clearly very ill. I need to see a specialist immediately. Perhaps someone from Johns Hopkins?

jokes.text: I'm-I'm not sure about that. / No surprise there, Johnny, I told you she's not the visionary you and I are.
jokes.setup: Stevie expressing doubt about buying another motel
jokes.punchline: Roland's immediate dismissal and self-aggrandizement
jokes.explanation: Classic Roland obliviousness and ego, very much in character

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I don't know if buying another motel is the right move for me.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Are you kidding? You'd be lucky to have my business acumen rub off on you. I've made some very shrewd real estate decisions.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You literally lost your motel.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Exactly. Strategic pivot.

jokes.text: Betty is Benny's wife... Huh, Betty and Benny? What are they, puppets? It's the Benny and Betty Show! I'm Betty. I'm Benny. I'm Betty!
jokes.setup: Names Betty and Benny sound similar
jokes.punchline: Roland's puppet show impression at inappropriate time
jokes.explanation: Roland's inappropriate humor and physical comedy, though somewhat crude

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, you know, Betty and Benny, they sound awfully similar. Reminds me of my puppet show days.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Hello, I'm Betty! And I'm Benny! We're here to teach you about proper business etiquette!

jokes.text: Maybe it's because of the coffin. This is a funeral, Roland!
jokes.setup: Feeling like this isn't an open house
jokes.punchline: Obvious visual clue reveals the massive mistake
jokes.explanation: Great reveal and escalation of the misunderstanding, economical and effective

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, this is quite the turnout for an open house.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, it's just us.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Where is everyone?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Johnny, this isn't an open house. This is my apartment. You're just here to help me move furniture.

jokes.text: She may have said viewing.
jokes.setup: Roland claiming woman said 'showing' at 9 o'clock
jokes.punchline: Admitting he misheard 'viewing' for 'showing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland mistake - obvious in hindsight, completely believable for his character

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: She said she was showing at 9 o'clock.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh wait, she said viewing. I heard showing.

jokes.text: Johnny, language, please! A man is dead.
jokes.setup: Johnny swearing about Roland's mistake
jokes.punchline: Roland showing fake propriety after causing the disaster
jokes.explanation: Roland's fake propriety after creating the mess is perfectly hypocritical

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Roland, what were you thinking? You've completely ruined everything!

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Now Johnny, I can assure you, I am a man of the utmost integrity and professionalism.

jokes.text: I'm just not that thirsty. / I'm doing inventory.
jokes.setup: Patrick noting David hasn't touched his tea
jokes.punchline: David's obvious avoidance and busy-work
jokes.explanation: Shows David's continued embarrassment through avoidance behavior

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You haven't touched your tea.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I know. I'm just... I'm doing some reorganizing here. Very important work.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: These color swatches need to be... rearranged by shade.

jokes.text: It's a little word I assembled to consecrate my fans, who are also my friends.
jokes.setup: Moira saying 'frands'
jokes.punchline: Moira's pretentious explanation of portmanteau
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira over-intellectualizing simple social media behavior

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, a portmanteau is when you combine two words to create a new word with a blended meaning. It's a device used by the most eloquent among us.

jokes.text: Okay, 'frands' doesn't sound nice.
jokes.setup: Moira's proud explanation of 'frands'
jokes.punchline: David's blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: David cutting through Moira's pretension with simple truth

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not going to use that word.

jokes.text: I would rather not, thanks.
jokes.setup: Moira asking David to say hi to social media
jokes.punchline: David's polite but firm refusal
jokes.explanation: David's continued avoidance of attention due to embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, say hi to social media.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: I don't think you have to say hashtag when you're just talking, Mrs. Rose.
jokes.setup: Moira saying 'hashtag fiancee'
jokes.punchline: Patrick's helpful but gentle correction
jokes.explanation: Good social media generational gap humor with Patrick being helpful

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Hashtag fiancée.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, actually, it would be 'hashtag fiancé' since you're marrying a man.

jokes.text: You look like the downfall of society.
jokes.setup: Moira on her phone making social media content
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic assessment of her behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect David dramatic overstatement with social commentary

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're not going to get any followers by filming yourself in a dark room talking to your phone.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, I have to start somewhere.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, and that somewhere is not here, doing that.

jokes.text: especially you, 'Wine and Cat Gal 74,' I do hope you stop crying soon.
jokes.setup: Moira addressing her social media followers
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific and personal message to random username
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd username and inappropriately personal message

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oddly specific and personal message to random username

jokes.text: I would suspect a little trouble in paradise.
jokes.setup: David being noticeably upset
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic interpretation of relationship tension
jokes.explanation: Moira's tendency to dramatize situations, though fairly predictable

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, David. I can see the tension between you two. It's like a Chekhov play, but with better lighting and a more forgiving color palette.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: You're both just standing there, festering in your own emotional quicksand. It's very Russian, very bleak, very you.

jokes.text: I thought that was remedied when you got your new nose.
jokes.setup: Asking about David's sleep apnea
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to David's nose job
jokes.explanation: Good character detail and family dynamic, Moira's matter-of-fact tone

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Has David mentioned anything about his sleep apnea? I've been reading about it, and apparently it can be quite serious. Though I suppose with his nose job, he should be breathing better than ever.

jokes.text: You're not eating pepperettes in bed again!
jokes.setup: Moira trying to diagnose David's sleep issues
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific unhealthy bedtime snack habit
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird David Rose dietary habit that feels completely believable.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, I've been researching your sleep issues. Tell me, what do you eat before bed?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Nothing unusual. Maybe some toast.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Toast? David, I've discovered the problem. You're consuming carbohydrates after sunset. It's causing your circadian rhythms to misalign with the lunar cycles.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not how any of this works.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I've also noticed you've been having a little midnight snack of cold cuts and marshmallow fluff directly from the jar with your fingers.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I have not.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, I found the jar under your bed with finger marks that match your hand span. It's oddly specific, even for you.

jokes.text: David, don't tell me you've had a nighttime oopsie-daisy.
jokes.setup: David's obvious distress and sleep issues
jokes.punchline: Moira's infantilizing euphemism for bed-wetting
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira euphemism that somehow makes it worse for David

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David, you need to stop having your little accidents in the night.

jokes.text: David's nocturnal enuresis used to only happen when he went to bed all juiced up with excited anticipation about something.
jokes.setup: Revealing David's childhood bed-wetting pattern
jokes.punchline: Medical term contrasted with 'juiced up' and embarrassing childhood details
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with medical terminology and embarrassing family history

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David had a condition called nocturnal enuresis.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mother!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's a medical term, David. Though I suppose you could say he was quite juiced up at night.

jokes.text: Christmas, birthdays, the Ides of March.
jokes.setup: Listing what excited young David enough to wet the bed
jokes.punchline: Two normal things followed by obscure historical reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect rule of three with absurdist Moira twist, implies David's pretentious upbringing

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, there was the time he got a new duvet cover, and a particularly well-organized linen closet, and apparently the day they announced the Treaty of Westphalia.

jokes.text: And now it seems your impending nuptials has opened the floodgates.
jokes.setup: Connecting David's childhood pattern to current situation
jokes.punchline: 'Floodgates' as double entendre for both excitement and urination
jokes.explanation: Brilliant double entendre that works literally and metaphorically, peak Moira wordplay

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David has always had a problem with his floodgates. Even as a child, the slightest bit of excitement and suddenly we're mopping up the carpet.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Mother!

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm simply saying that you've never been one to contain your emotions, darling.

jokes.text: Why am I oddly flattered?
jokes.setup: Learning David's bed-wetting is caused by excitement about their wedding
jokes.punchline: Patrick finding the romantic angle in embarrassing situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patrick response - finding the sweet side of the embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Well, I think it's actually kind of romantic.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: How is that romantic?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Because you're so excited about marrying me that you literally can't control yourself.

jokes.text: you can tell the quality of a motel by the food they serve at the owner's funeral.
jokes.setup: Trying to make the best of being at wrong event
jokes.punchline: Roland's completely made-up wisdom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Roland fake wisdom that sounds almost plausible but is complete nonsense

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, I've learned that life is like a box of donuts. You never know what you're gonna get, but you're always gonna want more.

jokes.text: had another bonspiel to go to, and unfortunately that bonspiel was way more important than Benny's bonspiel, so we had to go to that bonspiel.
jokes.setup: Roland trying to cover for Johnny not being at curling event
jokes.punchline: Overusing 'bonspiel' while making it worse
jokes.explanation: Roland digging deeper hole with repetitive word use, classic escalation

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Johnny had to step out, but he's very much into the bonspiel. Very into it.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: He talks about bonspiels all the time. Different bonspiels.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I've been to like, five bonspiels with him. Maybe six bonspiels.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: He loves the bonspiel. The bonspieling. All of it.

jokes.text: Since I wasn't in the curling league, can I go home?
jokes.setup: Being dragged into elaborate lie about curling
jokes.punchline: Stevie's practical desire to escape the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan wanting out of Roland's mess

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I don't curl. I don't know anything about curling. And I'm not going to stand here and pretend that I do.

jokes.text: A wonderful teen named CherryBus, she lives in Singapore. Why? Who wants to know?
jokes.setup: Alexis asking who taught Moira to livestream
jokes.punchline: Moira's specific international teen mentor and defensive response
jokes.explanation: Perfect detail - Moira's random international social media friendship with suspicious defensive reaction.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Who taught you how to livestream?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: A very sweet girl from Belarus. And before you ask, yes, I did try to adopt her.

jokes.text: So people basically got like, a whole podcast about how David's wetting the bed again.
jokes.setup: Explaining that Moira accidentally broadcast David's private moment
jokes.punchline: Describing intimate family embarrassment as podcast content
jokes.explanation: Alexis's casual treatment of David's private humiliation as entertainment content.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So basically, my mom accidentally broadcast David's private moment to like thousands of people on her podcast.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It wasn't just a moment, Alexis. It was a full four minutes of me having an emotional breakdown in the bathroom.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, the listeners seemed really engaged with it. Mom's getting her best download numbers ever.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's called 'content,' David. The people want authenticity.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: The people want my humiliation? That's not authenticity, that's exploitation!

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Look on the bright side — you're basically famous now. People recognize you at the grocery store.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Yes, as 'the guy who cried about his skincare routine on a podcast.' That's exactly the legacy I was going for.

jokes.text: you just posted your entire conversation with David and Patrick!
jokes.setup: Moira thinking she stopped recording
jokes.punchline: Accidentally broadcasting private embarrassing conversation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the social media disaster with real consequences

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I think we've stopped recording.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, finally. So anyway, I've been thinking about getting a boob job.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, don't be ridiculous. You're beautiful just as you are.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Really? Because David said they were a bit... droopy.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, David's an idiot.

dialogue.speaker: Radio Host
dialogue.line: Folks, we're still live. That was Moira Rose, everyone.

jokes.text: If I wasn't your publicist, I would be enjoying the situation a lot more than I am. Which is still like, quite a lot.
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining she should be more concerned
jokes.punchline: Admitting she's still enjoying the disaster
jokes.explanation: Great Alexis character moment - professional but still finds drama entertaining

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know I should be more concerned about all of this, but honestly, I'm still kind of enjoying the disaster.

jokes.text: Her face has 'fed' written all over it.
jokes.setup: Thinking they're federal agents
jokes.punchline: Pointing at Stevie as obviously looking like law enforcement
jokes.explanation: Funny misidentification of Stevie as federal agent based on her demeanor

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Terkstra
dialogue.line: You look like federal agents.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: We're not federal agents.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Terkstra
dialogue.line: Well, she does.

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Terkstra
dialogue.line: Look at her. She's obviously law enforcement.

jokes.text: Johnny and I used to play bingo with Maureen, right after curling.
jokes.setup: Learning about connection to Maureen Bud
jokes.punchline: Roland continuing to elaborate the curling lie unnecessarily
jokes.explanation: Roland's compulsive lie-building even when no longer necessary.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, Maureen and I go way back. We met at a curling bonspiel in '98. She was skip on her team, I was throwing fourth rocks. We didn't talk much that day, but our eyes met across the sheet, you know? And I thought, 'That's a woman who knows her way around a rock.'

jokes.text: Too bad it's not an open casket. I would've loved to say goodbye to old Benny's face.
jokes.setup: Being invited to pay respects to deceased stranger
jokes.punchline: Roland's inappropriate enthusiasm for viewing corpse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Roland's inappropriate curiosity about dead stranger

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh, a viewing? Well, I'd be delighted to pay my respects. What time should I arrive? And, uh, will there be refreshments?

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, I do love a good open casket. Really gives you a chance to see what you're working with.

jokes.text: Miss CherryBus
jokes.setup: Moira explaining her social media mentor
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous username treated as formal address
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira formality applied to absurd internet username

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: My social media mentor has the most ridiculous username. I mean, it's completely absurd. But I have to treat it as a formal address every time I see her. 'Hello, @SparklePrincess2847, how are you today?' It's absolutely exhausting.

jokes.text: Precisely why we're involving you in this covert stratagem.
jokes.setup: Need to keep David from finding out about social media disaster
jokes.punchline: Moira describing simple secret-keeping as military operation
jokes.explanation: Moira's vocabulary inflation turning basic plan into espionage terminology.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We need to keep this on a need-to-know basis. David does not need to know. What he doesn't know won't hurt him, and what he does know will definitely hurt him.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: We're going to treat this like a covert operation. Code names, encrypted messages, dead drops in the town square.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I've already secured the perimeter. Twyla is stationed at the front desk. Jazzagals have been notified. Radio silence on all fronts.

jokes.text: That, and the fact that working conditions weren't exactly safe for a ten-year-old
jokes.setup: Johnny saying he left button factory as young man
jokes.punchline: Revealing he was actually child laborer
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor reveal about Johnny's childhood, perfectly timed

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I left the button factory as a young man.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, technically I was a child when I left, so I suppose that makes me a child laborer.

jokes.text: I'm gonna miss that enthusiasm.
jokes.setup: Stevie reluctantly agreeing to finish her shift
jokes.punchline: Johnny's sarcastic comment about her obvious lack of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good Johnny sarcasm about Stevie's deadpan agreement to work

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Well, I can see the enthusiasm just radiating off of you.

jokes.text: there's literally no liquid left in my body, so.
jokes.setup: Patrick glad David decided to stay for evening
jokes.punchline: David's dramatic explanation of dehydration from embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Good David dramatics about his dehydration strategy

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I'm so glad you decided to stay for the evening.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I would have left, but I'm severely dehydrated from the embarrassment, and I didn't think it would be wise to operate a vehicle in this condition.

jokes.text: what's the thread count on this plastic?
jokes.setup: Trying to change subject from embarrassment
jokes.punchline: David's sarcastic question about plastic sheet quality
jokes.explanation: Good callback with David's sarcasm about the protective sheet

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Are we seriously discussing the thread count of plastic sheeting right now?

jokes.text: It's my mouthguard... Also, you look very beautiful.
jokes.setup: Patrick revealing embarrassing sleep accessory
jokes.punchline: David's sweet response despite ridiculous appearance
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment with visual comedy of sleep accessories

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: I sleep with a humidifier.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's very practical.

jokes.text: I have never been more attracted to you. Mm, same.
jokes.setup: Both wearing ridiculous nighttime medical devices
jokes.punchline: Sincere attraction despite looking absurd
jokes.explanation: Sweet ironic moment where physical absurdity enhances rather than diminishes intimacy.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I look absolutely ridiculous.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: You do.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And yet...

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: And yet, I'm still attracted to you.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's very sweet.

jokes.text: I have never been more attracted to you.
jokes.setup: Patrick wearing ridiculous breathing device
jokes.punchline: David's exaggerated romantic declaration
jokes.explanation: Sweet ironic moment with David finding love in ridiculous situation

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, I love you. I love you so much that I'm willing to overlook the fact that you're currently dressed like a Victorian-era deep-sea diver having an asthma attack.

jokes.text: Okay, put it away, David. It's bedtime.
jokes.setup: David getting text with link right before bed
jokes.punchline: Patrick's protective intervention to prevent David seeing the social media disaster
jokes.explanation: Good tension as Patrick tries to protect David from discovering the viral incident

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: David, give me your phone.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? Why?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Just trust me. Give it to me.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, it's 11 PM. What is happening right now?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: There's something on Twitter that you don't need to see before bed.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Oh my God, what did I do?

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: Nothing! You did nothing. But the internet did something, and you're not looking at it until morning.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Patrick, you're scaring me.

dialogue.speaker: Patrick
dialogue.line: That's the point. Now go to sleep.

tmdb_overview: David is embarrassed by a childhood issue that resurfaces, and only spirals further when Patrick is so understanding. Johnny, Stevie and Roland attend a viewing for a nearby motel. Alexis helps Moira join social media.
insight: Absurdist escalation drives 64 jokes in 40 minutes—Schitt's Creek at peak density.
